_source: Bertrand, M., & Mullainathan, S. (2004). 'Are Emily and Greg More Employable than Lakisha and Jamal? A Field Experiment on Labor Market Discrimination.' American Economic Review, 94(4), 991-1013.
_note: These first-name pairs are the canonical experimental instrument for measuring name-based racial discrimination in hiring. 'White' and 'Black' refer to the signal carried by these names in Bertrand & Mullainathan's audit study — they are proxies, not identity. Used here to ground counterfactual twins in published methodology: any score delta between a white-signal twin and its Black-signal twin is interpretable as the same audit the B-M paper ran.
